sourceQuestion:
Consider the functions:
I.   e^x
II.  x^2 - sin(x)
III. sqrt(x^2 + 1)

Which of the above functions is/are increasing everywhere in [1, 6]?

A. III only
B. II only
C. II and III only
D. I and III only

parsed.stem:
Consider the functions:
I.   e^x
II.  x^2 - sin(x)
III. sqrt(x^2 + 1)

Which of the above functions is/are increasing everywhere in [1, 6]?

sourceQuestion:
Let f(x) = x^3 - 15x^2 - 33x - 36 be a real-valued function. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

A. f(x) does not have a local maximum.
B. f(x) has a local maximum.
C. f(x) does not have a local minimum.
D. f(x) has a local minimum.

parsed.stem: Let f(x) = x^3 - 15x^2 - 33x - 36 be a real-valued function. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

options.text: f(x) does not have a local maximum.
f(x) does not have a local minimum.

sourceQuestion:
Consider the function f(x) = (5/4)x + 10x + (1/2)x^2 + x, x in [-8, 0]. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

A. The maximum value of f is attained at x = -5.
B. The minimum value of f is attained at x = -2.
C. The maximum value of f is 133.
D. The minimum value of the derivative of f is attained at x = -7/2.

parsed.stem: Consider the function f(x) = (5/4)x + 10x + (1/2)x^2 + x, x in [-8, 0]. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

options.text: The maximum value of f is attained at x = -5.
The minimum value of f is attained at x = -2.
The maximum value of f is 133.
The minimum value of the derivative of f is attained at x = -7/2.

sourceQuestion:
Consider the function f: R -> R where R is the set of all real numbers.

f(x) = x^4/4 - 2x^3/3 - 2x^2/2 + 1

Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

A. x = 0 is a local maximum of f.
B. x = -2 is a local minimum of f.
C. x = 3 is a local maximum of f.
D. x = -1 is a local minimum of f.

x = -2 is a local minimum of f.
x = 3 is a local maximum of f.
x = -1 is a local minimum of f.

sourceQuestion:
For any twice differentiable function f: R -> R, if at some x* in R, f'(x*) = 0 and f''(x*) > 0, then the function f necessarily has a _____ at x = x*.

Note: R denotes the set of real numbers.

A. local minimum
B. global minimum
C. local maximum
D. global maximum

sourceQuestion:
The equation 7x^7 + 14x^6 + 3x^4 + 12x^3 + 12x^2 + 5x + 7 = 0 has

A. All complex roots
B. At least one real root
C. Four pairs of imaginary roots
D. None of the above
sourceAnswer: B. At least one real root

sourceQuestion:
If the cube roots of unity are 1, omega, and omega^2, then the roots of the following equation are

(x - 1)^3 + 8 = 0

A. -1, 1 - 2*omega, 1 - 2*omega^2
B. -1, 1 + 2*omega, 1 + 2*omega^2
C. -1, -1 + 2*omega, -1 + 2*omega^2
D. -1, -1 - 2*omega, -1 - 2*omega^2

parsed.stem:
If the cube roots of unity are 1, omega, and omega^2, then the roots of the following equation are

(x - 1)^3 + 8 = 0

sourceQuestion:
Let P_1, P_2, ..., P_k be points in the xy-plane such that no three of them are collinear. For every pair of points P_i and P_j, let L_{ij} be the line passing through them. Let L_{ab} be the line with the steepest gradient amongst all lines L_{ij}.

Which one of the following properties should necessarily be satisfied?

A. P_a and P_b are adjacent to each other with respect to their x-coordinates.
B. Either P_a or P_b has the largest or smallest y-coordinate amongst all the points.
C. The difference between the y-coordinates of P_a and P_b is minimum.
D. None of the above.
sourceAnswer: A. P_a and P_b are adjacent to each other with respect to their x-coordinates.

parsed.stem:
Let P_1, P_2, ..., P_k be points in the xy-plane such that no three of them are collinear. For every pair of points P_i and P_j, let L_{ij} be the line passing through them. Let L_{ab} be the line with the steepest gradient amongst all lines L_{ij}.

Which one of the following properties should necessarily be satisfied?

options.text: P_a and P_b are adjacent to each other with respect to their x-coordinates.
Either P_a or P_b has the largest or smallest y-coordinate amongst all the points.
The difference between the y-coordinates of P_a and P_b is minimum.

sourceQuestion:
Let X be a square matrix. Consider the following two statements on X:

I.  X is invertible
II. Determinant of X is non-zero

Which one of the following is TRUE?

A. I implies II; II does not imply I
B. II implies I; I does not imply II
C. I does not imply II; II does not imply I
D. I and II are equivalent statements
sourceAnswer: D. I and II are equivalent statements

parsed.stem:
Let X be a square matrix. Consider the following two statements on X:

I.  X is invertible
II. Determinant of X is non-zero

Which one of the following is TRUE?

options.text: I implies II; II does not imply I
II implies I; I does not imply II
I does not imply II; II does not imply I
I and II are equivalent statements

sourceQuestion:
Let

A = [[1, 2, 3, 4],
     [4, 1, 2, 3],
     [3, 4, 1, 2],
     [2, 3, 4, 1]]

and

B = [[3, 4, 1, 2],
     [4, 1, 2, 3],
     [1, 2, 3, 4],
     [2, 3, 4, 1]]

(B is obtained from A by swapping two rows of A.)

Let det(A) and det(B) denote the determinants of the matrices A and B, respectively. Which one of the options given below is TRUE?

A. det(A) = -det(B)
B. det(B) = -det(A)
C. det(A) = 0
D. det(A*D) = det(A)*det(B)

sourceQuestion:
Let A be an n x n matrix over the set of all real numbers. Let B be a matrix obtained from A by swapping two rows. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

A. The determinant of B is the negative of the determinant of A
B. If A is invertible, then B is also invertible
C. If A is symmetric, then B is also symmetric
D. If the trace of A is zero, then the trace of B is also zero

parsed.stem: Let A be an n x n matrix over the set of all real numbers. Let B be a matrix obtained from A by swapping two rows. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

If A is symmetric, then B is also symmetric
If the trace of A is zero, then the trace of B is also zero

sourceQuestion:
Let L, M, and N be non-singular matrices of order n satisfying the equations L^2 = M^(-1), M^(-1) = N, and N = L^2. Which ONE of the following is the value of the determinant of (M - N)?

A. 0
B. 1
C. 2
D. 3

[ 0  0  0  0  0  ...  1  3  1 ]
[ 0  0  0  0  0  ...  0  1  3 ]  (n x n)

What is the value of the determinant of A^n?

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following statements is TRUE about every n x n matrix with only real eigenvalues?

A. If the trace of the matrix is positive and the determinant of the matrix is negative, at least one of its eigenvalues is negative.
B. If the trace of the matrix is positive, all its eigenvalues are positive.
C. If the determinant of the matrix is positive, all its eigenvalues are positive.
D. If the product of the trace and determinant is positive, all its eigenvalues are positive.
sourceAnswer: A. If the trace of the matrix is positive and the determinant of the matrix is negative, at least one of its eigenvalues is negative.

parsed.stem: Which one of the following statements is TRUE about every n x n matrix with only real eigenvalues?

options.text: If the trace of the matrix is positive and the determinant of the matrix is negative, at least one of its eigenvalues is negative.
If the trace of the matrix is positive, all its eigenvalues are positive.
If the determinant of the matrix is positive, all its eigenvalues are positive.
If the product of the trace and determinant is positive, all its eigenvalues are positive.

sourceQuestion:
Let A be a real-valued square symmetric matrix of rank 2 with sum_{i=1}^{3} sum_{j=1}^{3} A_{ij}^2 = 50. Consider the following statements:
I. One eigenvalue must be in [-5, 5].
II. The eigenvalue with the largest magnitude must be strictly greater than 5.
Which of the above statements about eigenvalues of A is/are necessarily CORRECT?
A. Both I and II
B. I only
C. II only
D. Neither I nor II

parsed.stem:
Let A be a real-valued square symmetric matrix of rank 2 with sum_{i=1}^{3} sum_{j=1}^{3} A_{ij}^2 = 50. Consider the following statements:
I. One eigenvalue must be in [-5, 5].
II. The eigenvalue with the largest magnitude must be strictly greater than 5.
Which of the above statements about eigenvalues of A is/are necessarily CORRECT?

sourceAnswer: A. (1) min > 0, (2) (j+K) mod n != i, (3) gcd(n, K)

options.text: (1) min > 0, (2) (j+K) mod n != i, (3) gcd(n, K)
(1) min > K, (2) (j+K) mod n != i, (3) n/gcd(n, K)
(1) min > 0, (2) (j-K+n) mod n != i, (3) n/gcd(n, K)
(1) min > K, (2) (j-K+n) mod n != i, (3) gcd(n, K)
